sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after clicking on the red dot at (9, 52).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

print(f"Before: {len(before['nodes'])} nodes, After: {len(after['nodes'])} nodes")

# Find differences
changed = []
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['id'] in before_nodes:
        bn = before_nodes[n['id']]
        if n['hash'] != bn['hash'] or n['pixels'] != bn['pixels'] or n['children'] != bn['children']:
            changed.append(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels {bn['pixels']}->{n['pixels']}")
    else:
        changed.append(f"NEW node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

for n in before['nodes']:
    if n['id'] not in after_nodes:
        changed.append(f"GONE node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}")

for c in changed:
    print(c)

# Check if the red dot at (9,52) is still there
print("\nNodes near (9,52):")
for n in after['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    if any(8 <= r <= 10 and 51 <= c <= 53 for r, c in b):
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, boundary={b}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

</parameter>
